evidence.scientific_clarity: Script correctly builds a stacked bar with 4 classes across 5 conditions, y-axis 0-1 with percent labels, fully matching the brief.
evidence.typography: No pdf.fonttype=42 set; pdffonts confirms Type 3 DejaVuSans with no ToUnicode map — text is not selectable or editable; DejaVu Sans default not overridden.
evidence.palette: Tableau-inspired 4-color qualitative palette is mostly harmonious, but green (#59a14f) and red (#e15759) pair is problematic for red-green colorblind viewers.
evidence.layout_density: figsize=(6,4) with tight_layout(), bar width 0.6, top/right spines removed, y-range exactly 0-1 — well-proportioned with no wasted space.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches figure content and legend placement (upper-right, no frame) is clean, but caption contains the odd phrase 'no data were mocked' and lacks a specific numerical finding.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF confirmed as Type 3 (bitmap-embedded) DejaVuSans with no /ToUnicode; script never sets matplotlib.rcParams['pdf.fonttype']=42, so text is not selectable.
evidence.file_format: All required files present and non-trivial: figure.pdf (12 KB), figure.png (26 KB), figure.svg (30 KB), caption.tex (222 B), gen_figure.py (1.5 KB).
evidence.caption_quality: Caption correctly describes the chart type, classes (A-D), conditions, and 100% normalization, but omits any specific numerical claim or finding about the data.

overall_strengths: Clean, well-proportioned layout with appropriate axis formatting, percent labels, and minimal chartjunk (spines removed, tight layout).
All required output files are present and non-trivial; the generation script is concise and correctly implements the brief's stacked-bar specification.
overall_weaknesses: pdf.fonttype=42 is never set, resulting in Type 3 (non-selectable, non-editable) DejaVu Sans fonts in the PDF — a standard requirement for journal submission.
Caption lacks a specific numerical finding and includes the non-standard phrase 'no data were mocked'; the red/green palette pair is a colorblind accessibility risk.
